author: Mohammed Thanseer
description: Embed ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok, DeepSeek, Kimi, Copliot, Manus and Perplexity in a sidebar. Send vault notes and selected text directly into AI chat. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Mohammed Thansheer
manifest.description: An embedded browser for any AI tool — ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok, DeepSeek and more. Send vault notes as context and save responses directly into your vault.
manifest.name: OmniChat

name: OmniChat

releases.author: THANSHEER
releases.description:
- New: auto-reconstruct code blocks and tables when saving AI responses, with a settings toggle
- New: "Insert at cursor" as a third save destination, alongside new note / append
- New: source service + save date added as frontmatter on new notes
- New: "Save AI response" command for the command palette
- Improved: context search now loads note content lazily, instead of indexing the whole vault upfront

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at 1.4.2 -->

## What's Changed
### Other Changes
* directive comment bug fixed by @THANSHEER in https://github.com/THANSHEER/obsidian-omnichat/pull/15


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/THANSHEER/obsidian-omnichat/compare/1.4.1...1.4.2
releases.name: v1.4.2 — Smart save formatting & insert-at-cursor

releases.author: THANSHEER
releases.description:
New Template Variables

Two new variables are now available in prompt templates:
- {{path}} — inserts the active note's full vault path
- {{tags}} — inserts all tags (inline + frontmatter) from the active note, comma-separated

Clear All Context Command

"Clear all context" is now available as a command palette entry (OmniChat: Clear all context), letting you clear context from the keyboard without opening the panel.

Confirmation Before Clearing Context

Clicking "Clear all" in the context panel now shows a confirmation dialog so accidental clears don't lose your context list.

Configurable Default Split Panel Service

You can now choose which AI service opens by default in the split panel — find it under OmniChat settings.

---
Bug Fixes

- Duplicate custom URL guard — Adding a custom service with a URL that already exists now shows a warning instead of silently creating a duplicate.
- Save filename collisions — Saved AI response filenames now include milliseconds (HH-MM-SS-mmm), preventing overwrites when saving quickly in succession.
- Cycle service notice — Running "Cycle service" when no services are enabled now shows a clear notice instead of silently doing nothing.
- Context size label — The size hint in the context panel now correctly reads "bytes" instead of "chars".
- Removed spoofed user agent — The hardcoded Windows user agent string has been removed from the webview; sites now see the real Electron user agent.

---
Internal Improvements

- Removed empty onunload() method
- Simplified SaveDestinationModal.appendToExisting into a named async method
- Fixed version-bump.mjs silently skipping versions.json updates when the key was missing
- Removed stale feature-tracking comments throughout the codebase

releases.author: THANSHEER
releases.description:
## What's changed

### Fixes
- Ribbon icon tooltip and notice text updated from "AI portal" to "OmniChat"
- README title now matches the plugin name in manifest.json (fixes Obsidian plugin validator warning)

### Security
- Upgraded vitest from 2.x to 4.1.8, resolving 5 vulnerabilities (4 moderate, 1 critical) in the esbuild/vite dependency chain

### Internal
- Package renamed from `aibrowser-chat` to `obsidian-omnichat`
- Removed unused internal state field (`isLoading`) from the view layer

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at 1.3.0 -->

## What's Changed
### Bug Fixes
* Rename AI Portal to OmniChat by @THANSHEER in https://github.com/THANSHEER/obsidian-omnichat/pull/10
### Other Changes
* Bug/bug fixes by @THANSHEER in https://github.com/THANSHEER/obsidian-omnichat/pull/12


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/THANSHEER/obsidian-omnichat/compare/1.2.0...1.3.0

releases.author: THANSHEER
releases.description:
New Features

Prompt Templates
Create one-click prompts in Settings that instantly inject text into the active AI service — no more retyping repeated instructions. Add, edit, and remove templates from the new Prompt Templates section in settings.

Split Panel
Open a second AI service side-by-side with the primary portal via the new Open split panel button in settings. Each panel tracks its own active URL independently.

Context & Notes Controls
- Auto-add on open — automatically attaches the current note to context when the portal opens
- Strip frontmatter — removes YAML --- blocks before sending notes as context
- Auto-clear context — clears context items after each send
- Context prefix — prepend custom text to every context send
- Max context length — configurable character limit (default 50,000)

Improvements

- Rewrote the view layer in vanilla TypeScript — dropped the React/react-dom dependency, reducing bundle size
- Replaced the old AIChatPanel.tsx with a cleaner AIChatView.ts that renders natively inside Obsidian's workspace
- Consolidated settings UI: service toggles now loop over a config array instead of manual repetition

Security

- Fixed DOM-based XSS: user-supplied URLs are now validated and sanitized through the URL parser before being assigned to the webview — only http: and https: protocols are permitted

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at 1.2.0 -->

## What's Changed
### New Features
* Feat/added prompt template by @THANSHEER in https://github.com/THANSHEER/AI-Browser-Chat/pull/8
### Other Changes
* Feat/new release by @THANSHEER in https://github.com/THANSHEER/AI-Browser-Chat/pull/7
* Feat/added prompt template by @THANSHEER in https://github.com/THANSHEER/AI-Browser-Chat/pull/9


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/THANSHEER/AI-Browser-Chat/compare/1.1.2...1.2.0
releases.name: Added support to copilot, manus, kimi

releases.author: THANSHEER
releases.description:
## Bug fixes

  - **Restored full sidebar height for the embedded webview.** A regression
    introduced in v1.1.2 (commit `72cdee0`) changed the webview's CSS
    `display` from `inline-flex` to `block`, which collapsed its height to
    ~400-500px inside Obsidian's sidebar regardless of the available space.
    The webview now correctly fills the leaf top-to-bottom again.
  - **Tightened the top padding** of the service-selector row so the header
    no longer leaves visible dead space above the dropdown.

  ## Why this happened

  Electron's `<webview>` is a custom element with no intrinsic content
  height. Inside a flex column, `display: block` cannot resolve
  `height: 100%` because block children fall back to normal flow. The
  working layout (pre-v1.1.2) used `display: inline-flex`, which makes the
  webview itself a flex item that accepts the height passed down through
  the chain. A code comment has been added next to the webview rule to
  prevent this from being re-broken.

  ## Upgrade notes

  No settings or config changes required. After updating, reload Obsidian
  (or toggle the plugin off/on) to load the new bundle.
releases.name: Fix sidebar height regression & tighten header version 1.1.2

releases.author: THANSHEER
releases.description:
Two root-cause fixes for the UI issues visible after build.

  Webview height (large empty area below the AI panel):
  - Switched the browser section from a pure-flex height chain to
    absolute positioning (.ai-chat-browser-frame and
    .ai-chat-browser-host are now position: absolute; inset: 0)
  - Electron's <webview> cannot resolve height: 100% against a
    flex-computed parent; it needs an explicitly-dimensioned
    positioned ancestor — this provides that
  - .ai-chat-browser-shell remains a flex: 1 item so the header
    stays fixed and the browser fills all remaining space
  - Added flex-direction: column and !important to .ai-chat-root
    and .ai-chat-app to prevent Obsidian from collapsing the chain

  Header rows stacking vertically instead of inline:
  - Obsidian's base CSS overrides display on plugin elements inside
    .view-content, breaking the flex row layout
  - Added display: flex !important to .vc-header, .vc-service-row,
    .vc-context-bar, .vc-ctx-actions, and .vc-ctx-right
  - Added display: inline-flex !important to all button elements
    (.vc-ctx-btn, .vc-add-btn, .vc-reload-btn, .vc-ctx-count) and
    width: auto; height: auto; box-sizing: border-box to prevent
    Obsidian's button styles from stretching them full-width
  - Added flex-wrap: nowrap to .vc-service-row and .vc-context-bar
    so items can never wrap to a new line regardless of sidebar width
  - Fixed .vc-svc-dot with display: block and min-width/min-height
    so Obsidian cannot collapse the service indicator dot
  - Added min-width to .vc-reload-btn and .vc-loading-pip to
    prevent flex shrinking squeezing them off-screen
releases.name: feat: 1.1.0 — context menu, toggle command, settings, UI fixes, and    docs

releases.author: THANSHEER
releases.description:
AI Browser Chat  1.0.0
Embed ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok, DeepSeek, and Perplexity in a persistent Obsidian sidebar — and send vault notes directly into the AI's chat input without leaving Obsidian.
Features
* Six AI services — ChatGPT, Claude, DeepSeek, Perplexity, Google Gemini, and Grok in one panel. Each can be individually enabled/disabled.
* Send selected text — Highlight any text in a note and run
* AI Browser Chat : Send selected text to AI* from the command palette.
* Vault context injection — Add the active note, all open tabs, any note, or an entire folder as context. Content is injected into the chat input (clipboard fallback if injection is unavailable).
* Context prefix — Prepend a custom instruction to every context send.
* Auto-clear context — Optionally clear the context list after each send.
* Default service — Choose which AI opens when the sidebar loads.
* Context memory — Selected files/folders persist across service switches and Obsidian restarts.
* Idle auto-refresh — Configurable silent reload keeps the AI session fresh.
* Open on startup — Restore the sidebar automatically when Obsidian loads.
Requirements
* Obsidian ≥ 0.15.0
* Desktop only (macOS, Windows, Linux) — uses Electron <webview>
Notes
* No API keys, no backend, no data collection. ChatPortal opens the real AI websites in an embedded browser using your existing session.
* Context injection is best-effort; clipboard copy is always the guaranteed fallback.
This is an unofficial plugin — not affiliated with OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, xAI, DeepSeek, or Perplexity.
releases.name: AI Browser Chat 1.0.0 release
